page: Amazon Web Services t2.nano is a burstable performance instance belonging to the t2 family. It features a single virtual CPU powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor clocked at 2.4 GHz, utilizing the i386 architecture. The instance is provisioned with 0.5 GB of system memory, which equates to 0.5 GB per core. It operates on the Xen hypervisor, does not include any local storage, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. It also does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data highlights the burstable nature and resource constraints of this instance. In single-core CPU performance tests using stress-ng, the server achieves a score of 1850.89, placing it in the average category. However, due to its single-core configuration, its multi-core CPU performance is also 1850.89, which falls into the bottom 10% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth for cached small blocks (16 KB) is measured at 76.09 GB/sec, and the Passmark CPU score is 1428.49, both of which are in the bottom 10%. Database and memory operations on Passmark register at 0.0, while Redis SET operations reach 0.52 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is limited to 1.1 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t2.nano represents a low-resource entry-level tier designed for workloads that do not require continuous high CPU utilization. The burstable CPU allocation model allows the instance to handle occasional spikes in demand while maintaining a highly economical resource profile. Due to the severe memory constraint of 0.5 GB and the lack of local storage, this instance is suited only for lightweight applications, basic microservices, low-traffic static web serving, or simple development and testing environments.
description: Amazon Web Services t2.nano is a burstable, entry-level instance featuring 1 vCPU powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor at 2.4 GHz on the i386 architecture. Equipped with 0.5 GB of memory, the instance operates on the Xen hypervisor, lacks local storage, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate limited processing capacity, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1850.89 (average) but a multi-core score and Passmark CPU score (1428.49) falling into the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is 76.09 GB/sec, and static web serving throughput is 1.1 GB/sec. Given its burstable CPU allocation and minimal memory footprint, the t2.nano is a highly cost-efficient option for low-resource workloads. It is best suited for lightweight microservices, basic development environments, and low-traffic static web hosting.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t2.nano is a burstable instance with 1 vCPU, an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor, and 0.5 GB of RAM. It is designed for low-resource workloads and basic development tasks.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t2.nano is a burstable i386 instance with 1 vCPU, 0.5 GB RAM, and no local storage, designed for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A low-resource, burstable single-core instance designed for lightweight microservices, basic development tasks, and low-traffic static web hosting.

0.5 GB of system memory with no local storage included
Burstable CPU allocation model for handling occasional performance spikes
Average single-core performance but limited multi-core and memory bandwidth
